UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the HomeServ Connect application, encompassing customers, service providers (workers), and administrators. Stores core profile information and role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

firebaseUid.description: Unique identifier provided by Firebase Authentication, linking this profile to an external authentication record.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for communication and authentication (derived from Firebase).

role.description: The role of the user within the application (Customer, Worker, or Admin).

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the user.

address.description: The primary physical address of the user (e.g., home address for customers, base address for workers).

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the user's primary address, for location-based services.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the user's primary address, for location-based services.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

bio.description: A short biographical description, primarily for Workers to describe their experience and services.

status.description: The current status of the user profile (e.g., active, banned, pending_verification).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

ServiceCategory.title: ServiceCategory
ServiceCategory.description: Defines a category of service offered within the HomeServ Connect platform (e.g., Plumbing, Electrical, HVAC).

id.description: Unique identifier for the ServiceCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the service category (e.g., 'Plumbing').

description.description: A brief description of the services included in this category.

iconUrl.description: URL to an icon representing the service category.

WorkerService.title: WorkerService
WorkerService.description: Represents a specific service category that a Worker is skilled in, linking a UserProfile (Worker) to a ServiceCategory. This entity allows for storing additional details about the worker's proficiency in that specific service.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkerService entity (linking a worker to a specific service category).

workerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the worker. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N WorkerService)

serviceCategoryId.description: Reference to the ServiceCategory ID. (Relationship: ServiceCategory 1:N WorkerService)

experienceYears.description: The number of years of experience the worker has in this specific service category.

certificationUrls.description: An array of URLs to certification documents relevant to this service category for the worker.

ServiceRequest.title: ServiceRequest
ServiceRequest.description: Represents a service request posted by a Customer, detailing the job required, its status, and associated worker and ratings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ServiceRequest entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the customer who created the service request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ServiceRequest)

workerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the worker who accepted and is performing the service request. Null if not yet accepted. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ServiceRequest)

serviceCategoryId.description: Reference to the ServiceCategory ID for this request. (Relationship: ServiceCategory 1:N ServiceRequest)

title.description: A concise title for the service request.

description.description: A detailed description of the service required, potentially assisted by AI.

jobLocationAddress.description: The specific street address where the service is to be performed.

jobLocationLatitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the job location.

jobLocationLongitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the job location.

status.description: The current status of the service request.

requestedDateTime.description: The date and time the customer has requested the service to be performed.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the service request was created.

acceptedAt.description: Timestamp when a worker accepted the service request.

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the worker started working on the service request.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the service request was marked as completed by the worker.

estimatedCost.description: An optional estimated cost for the service, either suggested by AI or entered by the customer.

actualCost.description: The final agreed-upon or actual cost of the completed service.

photoUrls.description: An array of URLs to photos related to the service request (e.g., before/after photos).

customerRating.description: Rating (1-5 stars) given by the worker to the customer after job completion.

customerReview.description: Text review provided by the worker about the customer after job completion.

workerRating.description: Rating (1-5 stars) given by the customer to the worker after job completion.

workerReview.description: Text review provided by the customer about the worker after job completion.

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Captures reports submitted by users regarding other users or service requests, for administrator review and action.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Report entity.

reporterUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user who submitted the report. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Report)

reportedUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the user being reported. Mutually exclusive with reportedServiceRequestId. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Report)

reportedServiceRequestId.description: Reference to the ServiceRequest ID being reported. Mutually exclusive with reportedUserId. (Relationship: ServiceRequest 1:N Report)

reason.description: A concise reason for the report (e.g., 'Fraud', 'No-show', 'Abusive Behavior').

details.description: Detailed explanation and context for the report.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the report was submitted.

status.description: The current processing status of the report by administrators.

resolutionNotes.description: Notes added by an administrator detailing the resolution or action taken for the report.

resolvedByAdminId.description: Reference to the UserProfile ID of the administrator who resolved the report. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Report)

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles (Customer, Worker, Admin). The `userId` in the path corresponds to the `firebaseUid` field within the document. Authorization: Users can read/write their own profile where `userId == request.auth.uid`. Admins can read all profiles and update specific fields like `status`. Includes denormalized 'firebaseUid' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user, serving as the document ID for their profile.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for enforcing Database Access Control (DBAC) for administrators. The document ID (`adminUid`) is the Firebase UID of an admin user. The mere existence of a document here grants admin privileges, enabling efficient and atomic role checks without relying on `get()` calls to the UserProfile collection, thus achieving authorization independence.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of an administrator, serving as the document ID.

definition.description: Master list of predefined service categories available on the platform. Publicly readable by all authenticated users. Writable only by administrators.

params.name: serviceCategoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a service category.

definition.description: Subcollection under a specific worker's profile, detailing the services that worker provides. Authorization: Only the worker whose `workerId` matches `request.auth.uid` can manage their own worker services. Includes denormalized 'workerId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: workerId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the worker, matching the parent UserProfile ID.

params.name: workerServiceId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific service offered by this worker.

definition.description: Stores all service requests created by customers. Authorization: Customers can manage their own requests (`customerId == request.auth.uid`). Workers can view available requests, accept them (`workerId == request.auth.uid` after acceptance), and update status. Includes denormalized 'customerId' and 'workerId' fields for authorization independence and QAP support.

params.name: serviceRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for a service request.

definition.description: Contains user-submitted reports about other users or service requests. Authorization: Any authenticated user can create a report (`reporterUserId == request.auth.uid`). Only administrators can read, update status, and add resolution notes. Includes denormalized 'reporterUserId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier for a submitted report.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
1.  **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** The user's `firebaseUid` is explicitly stored within the `UserProfile` document and also forms the document ID (`userId`). This means security rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `userId` in the path (e.g., `match /users/{userId} { allow read, write: if userId == request.auth.uid; }`) without needing to perform a `get()` operation on a parent document.
2.  **Worker Services (`/users/{workerId}/worker_services/{workerServiceId}`):** These are nested under the specific worker's profile. The `workerId` in the path directly corresponds to the `workerId` field within the `WorkerService` document. This denormalization ensures that rules can check ownership atomically (e.g., `allow write: if workerId == request.auth.uid;`) without `get()` calls.
3.  **Service Requests (`/service_requests/{serviceRequestId}`):** For service requests, both `customerId` (the creator) and `workerId` (the assigned worker) are directly embedded within the `ServiceRequest` document. This is crucial for collaborative data access, allowing rules to check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.customerId` or `request.auth.uid == resource.data.workerId` without external lookups, supporting atomic operations and simpler rule logic.
4.  **Admin Roles (`/roles_admin/{adminUid}`):** To enforce Database Access Control (DBAC) for administrators without `get()` calls, a dedicated top-level collection `roles_admin` is used. An admin's `firebaseUid` is stored as a document ID in this collection. Security rules can then check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` to determine admin status. This is an 'Existence over Content' approach, which is highly efficient and robust for Authorization Independence.
5.  **Reports (`/reports/{reportId}`):** The `reporterUserId` is directly present in the document for authorization purposes (e.g., a user can only read/delete their own reports if needed, though for reports, it's typically admin-only read/write).

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) Support:**
1.  **`/service_categories`:** This collection is designed for public read access (read-only for all authenticated users). It supports QAPs as any user can list all service categories without filtering requirements in the rules.
2.  **`/users/{userId}/worker_services`:** A worker can list *their own* worker services by querying `/users/$(request.auth.uid)/worker_services`. The path itself acts as the filter, making it a valid QAP.
3.  **`/service_requests`:** Customers can list their own service requests using `query.where('customerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. Workers can list available jobs using `query.where('status', '==', 'PENDING')` or jobs assigned to them using `query.where('workerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. These queries leverage explicit document fields and align with Firestore's query-ability, enabling efficient and secure QAPs.

**Structural Segregation:** Each collection maintains a homogeneous security posture. For instance, `service_categories` is for static, globally readable data. `users/{userId}/worker_services` is for data strictly owned by the `{userId}`. This prevents mixing different access requirements within a single collection, simplifying rule writing and reducing potential security vulnerabilities. The explicit `status` field in `UserProfile`, `ServiceRequest`, and `Report` facilitates clear state modeling and predictable schema enforcement.
